Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-272 
I am writing in response to your question as to whether my 
January 6, 1984, advice is altered by the changes made to 
Government Code Section 84308 in AB 2992. 
I have included 
copies of my advice letter to you and the bill. 
AB 2992 is 
designed to clarify Section 84308. 
There are no changes which 
alter my previous advice to you.

Very truly yours,

Statement. of economic Interest 
Bert Glennon, Jr.

9770 Culver Boulevard 
Culver City, CA 
90230 
Dear Mr. Glennon: 
322-5660 
322-5901 
322~' 
322-6<U4 
January 6, 1984 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-83-271 
Thank you for your request for advice on Government Code 
Section 84308(a).17 This section provides that: 
No ••• appointed officer ••• who serves on a 
quasi-judicial • • • commission, other than those 
governmental bodies specified in subdivision (d) ,2/ 
shall accept, solicit, or direc~ a contribution of 
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more, from any 
person, or his or her agent, who has an application for 
a license, permit, or other entitlement for use pending 
before any body of which the officer • • • is a member 
and for three months following the date a decision is 
rendered on the application or from any person, or his 
or her agent, who actively supports or opposes the, 
application; provided, however, that the officer ••• 
knows or has reason to know that the person who 
supports or opposes the application has a financial. 
1/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
11 Section 84308(d) states: 
Legislative bodies such as city councils, county 
boards of supervisors, and the State Legislature while 
acting as a body ora committee thereof are exempted 
from the coverage of this section •••• 
1I These terms are explained in the enclosed copy of 
2 cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.6. 


Bert Glennon, Jr. 
January 6, 1984 
Page 2 
interest •••• 
This prohibition shall apply regardless 
of whether the officer • • • accepts, solicits, or 
directs the contribution for himself • • • or on behalf 
of any other officer, alternate, or candidate for 
office. 
In your letter, you asked whether Section 84308(a) allows a 
planning commissioner to accept a contribution on behalf of a 
councilmember if the contributor is an applicant before the 
planning commission. 
The planning commissioner is the campaign 
manager for the councilmember. 
You asked whether the officer 
might be allowed to accept the contribution because it is on 
behalf of a candidate who is seeking a position that is exempt 
under Section 84308(d). 
The prohibitions of Section 84308 apply to contributions 
accepted, solicited, or directed, for the officer, himself, or 
- on behalf of any other candidate for office. 
There is no 
exception for contributions to candidates-for positions 
specified in Section 84308(d); Similarly, the regulation 
defining "solicited" and "directed" does not contain such an 
exception. 
(2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.6.) 
Thus, you are 
correct in concluding that the planning commissioner may not 
solicit or direct a contribution on_behalf of a councilmember. 
Assuming that the planning commissioner does not solicit ,or 
direct the contribution, Section 84308 does not prevent an 
applicant before the Planning Commission from making a direct 
contribution to the city councilmember, even though the planning 
commissioner/campaign manager may be aware of it. 
.. 
If you have any additional questions, please feel free to. 
contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
JSM:plh 
Enclosures 
Very tru~y yours,

sections.question: Whether Section 84308(a) allows a planning commissioner to accept a contribution on behalf of a councilmember if the contributor is an applicant before the planning commission, where the planning commissioner is the campaign manager for the councilmember, and whether the officer might be allowed to accept the contribution because it is on behalf of a candidate who is seeking a position that is exempt under Section 84308(d).
sections.conclusion: The prohibitions of Section 84308 apply to contributions accepted, solicited, or directed, for the officer, himself, or on behalf of any other candidate for office. There is no exception for contributions to candidates for positions specified in Section 84308(d). Thus, the planning commissioner may not solicit or direct a contribution on behalf of a councilmember. Assuming that the planning commissioner does not solicit or direct the contribution, Section 84308 does not prevent an applicant before the Planning Commission from making a direct contribution to the city councilmember, even though the planning commissioner/campaign manager may be aware of it.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a planning commissioner solicit or direct contributions on behalf of a city councilmember from applicants who have pending matters before the planning commission, when the councilmember's position may be exempt from Section 84308(d)?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A planning commissioner cannot solicit or direct contributions on behalf of a councilmember from applicants with pending matters before the commission. However, applicants may make direct contributions to the councilmember without the planning commissioner's solicitation, even if the planning commissioner is aware of them.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This document consists of two advice letters: the primary October 29, 1984 letter (A-84-272) responding to a follow-up question about AB 2992's impact on prior advice, and the original January 6, 1984 letter (A-83-271) containing the detailed analysis. The extraction focuses on the substantive advice from the January letter with context from the October follow-up.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Section 84308(a) allows a planning commissioner to accept a contribution on behalf of a councilmember if the contributor is an applicant before the planning commission, where the planning commissioner is the campaign manager for the councilmember, and whether the officer might be allowed to accept the contribution because it is on behalf of a candidate who is seeking a position that is exempt under Section 84308(d).

CONCLUSION: The prohibitions of Section 84308 apply to contributions accepted, solicited, or directed, for the officer, himself, or on behalf of any other candidate for office. There is no exception for contributions to candidates for positions specified in Section 84308(d). Thus, the planning commissioner may not solicit or direct a contribution on behalf of a councilmember. Assuming that the planning commissioner does not solicit or direct the contribution, Section 84308 does not prevent an applicant before the Planning Commission from making a direct contribution to the city councilmember, even though the planning commissioner/campaign manager may be aware of it.

embedding.summary: The FPPC clarifies that planning commissioners are prohibited from soliciting or directing contributions on behalf of city councilmembers from applicants with pending matters before the commission, regardless of whether the councilmember's position is exempt under Section 84308(d).
